current.text: Tuning G1 gait parameters for warehouse pick-and-place at 0.6 m/s — cut peak joint torque 18%, smoothed the swing-leg spike.

recent.text: Patched 12 G1s in a customer fleet to firmware 1.5.3 — UniPwn-disclosed BT attack surface closed. Verified pairing now requires the physical side-button hold.
Tracking intermittent Go2 IMU drift under cold-start. Suspect magnetometer calibration order — collecting 200 cold-boot logs from the customer fleet tonight.
Deployed the ACT locomotion policy to 4 customer Go2s. Threshold success went 67% to 89% at 8cm obstacles, 41% to 91% on push recovery.
Re-reading the LeRobot ACT paper for the third time. The temporal-ensembling trick is the whole reason it transfers without retraining.
Wrote the G1 arm trajectory planner. Quintic interpolation, 200 Hz control loop, 0.5mm end-effector precision on the bench.
Designing the B2 warehouse-patrol geofence. Convex hull over the LiDAR SLAM map with a 30cm buffer for pose uncertainty at 5cm map resolution.
Debugging a DDS multicast TTL=1 issue at one customer site. Their switch vendor confirmed they are silently resetting TTL on L2 forwarding — control packets were looping back to the host.
Bench-tested the unitree_sim_isaaclab G1 env. Sim-to-real transfer on flat ground hits 96% success before any on-robot fine-tuning.
Re-read the UniPwn 2025 disclosure carefully. The BT pairing flaw is bad but the firmware-upload-over-BT-service is the catastrophic primitive — fixed in 1.5.2 but check if your fleet is on 1.5.0.
Measured the G1 in sport mode at full pace — 1.4 m/s on the bench, not the 1.5 m/s in the marketing. Battery lasted 1h 47m, not the 5h standby figure.
Refactored the unitree_sdk2 wrapper to add a heartbeat-watchdog. If the control host misses 3 consecutive state frames at 200 Hz, the robot calls Damp() autonomously.
